name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework (AI 100-1)
disclaimer: Informative only. Not a substitute for reading the published standard.

entries.title: Govern function
entries.summary: Establishes organizational processes and structures to anticipate, identify, and manage AI risks in alignment with organizational values and policies.

entries.title: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices
entries.summary: Requires documented organizational policies and practices for AI risk management that reflect the organization's values and risk tolerance.

entries.title: Legal and regulatory compliance policies
entries.summary: Addresses policies ensuring the AI system complies with applicable legal and regulatory requirements throughout its lifecycle.

entries.title: Trustworthy AI characteristics integration
entries.summary: Ensures that trustworthy AI characteristics such as fairness, transparency, and accountability are integrated into organizational policies.

entries.title: Accountability structures
entries.summary: Defines clear roles, responsibilities, and lines of accountability for AI risk management across the organization.

entries.title: Roles and responsibilities
entries.summary: Specifies that roles and responsibilities for AI risk management are clearly defined and assigned to appropriate personnel.

entries.title: Training and awareness
entries.summary: Addresses workforce training and awareness programs to ensure personnel understand AI risks and their management responsibilities.

entries.title: Workforce diversity and AI risk
entries.summary: Promotes diverse and multidisciplinary teams to provide broad perspectives in AI risk identification and management.

entries.title: Organizational risk tolerance
entries.summary: Requires that organizational risk tolerances for AI are defined and communicated to guide risk management decisions.

entries.title: Engagement with stakeholders
entries.summary: Addresses processes for engaging relevant internal and external stakeholders in AI risk management decisions.

entries.title: Policies for third-party AI
entries.summary: Establishes governance practices for third-party AI components, services, and data used within the organization.

entries.title: Map function
entries.summary: Establishes context for framing AI risks by identifying and documenting the AI system's purpose, stakeholders, and potential impacts.

entries.title: System context and intended purpose
entries.summary: Requires documentation of the AI system's intended purpose, operational context, assumptions, and limitations.

entries.title: Intended purpose documentation
entries.summary: Specifies that the intended purpose of the AI system is clearly defined, documented, and communicated to relevant stakeholders.

entries.title: Interdisciplinary review of context
entries.summary: Promotes review of the AI system context by interdisciplinary teams including domain experts, ethicists, and affected communities.

entries.title: Classification and categorization
entries.summary: Requires categorization of the AI system according to its risk level, application domain, and potential for harm.

entries.title: Benefits and costs assessment
entries.summary: Addresses assessment of potential benefits and costs of the AI system to all stakeholders, including affected communities.

entries.title: Risk identification across lifecycle
entries.summary: Covers identification of risks at each phase of the AI lifecycle, from design through deployment and decommissioning.

entries.title: Impact assessment
entries.summary: Requires assessment of potential impacts including on individuals, groups, communities, organizations, and society.

entries.title: Measure function
entries.summary: Employs quantitative and qualitative methods to analyze, assess, benchmark, and monitor AI risks and their related impacts.

entries.title: Metrics and measurement approaches
entries.summary: Specifies that appropriate metrics and measurement methodologies are identified and applied to evaluate AI system trustworthiness.

entries.title: Measurement of trustworthy characteristics
entries.summary: Addresses quantitative and qualitative measurement of AI trustworthiness characteristics including accuracy, fairness, and transparency.

entries.title: AI system evaluation
entries.summary: Covers evaluation activities including testing, auditing, and assessment of the AI system against defined criteria.

entries.title: Test and evaluation planning
entries.summary: Requires planning for test and evaluation activities that are proportionate to the risk level and complexity of the AI system.

entries.title: Bias and fairness assessment
entries.summary: Addresses identification and measurement of bias in AI systems, including computational, statistical, and societal biases.

entries.title: Monitoring and continuous measurement
entries.summary: Specifies ongoing monitoring of AI system performance and risks after deployment to detect changes in behavior or context.

entries.title: Manage function
entries.summary: Allocates resources and implements plans to respond to and recover from identified AI risks on a regular basis.

entries.title: Risk prioritization and response
entries.summary: Requires prioritization of identified AI risks and selection of appropriate response strategies such as mitigation, transfer, or acceptance.

entries.title: Risk treatment plans
entries.summary: Specifies that risk treatment plans are developed and implemented for prioritized AI risks with assigned responsibilities and timelines.

entries.title: Risk response and recovery
entries.summary: Addresses processes for responding to realized AI risks including incident response, escalation, and recovery procedures.

entries.title: Communication of AI risks
entries.summary: Requires clear communication of AI risks to relevant stakeholders including deployers, users, and affected communities.

entries.title: Residual risk documentation
entries.summary: Addresses documentation and communication of residual risks that remain after risk treatment measures have been applied.
